Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Data: I need to process the event log table row by row, grouping by Case ID to calculate total duration and analyze specific activity gaps. Case 101: Start: 2024-03-01 08:00 End: 2024-03-01 10:15 Total Duration: 2 hours 15 minutes. Activities: Receive -> Triage (10m) -> Assign (10m) -> Investigate (40m) -> Resolve (1h) -> Close (15m). Escalation: No. Status: Fast. Case 102: ... Case 105: ... Status: Slowest.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Step 1: Identify Long Resolution Cases: Compare durations: 101: ~2.25 hours 102: ~25 hours 103: ~1.33 hours 104: ~24 hours 105: ~49 hours Conclusion: Cases 102, 104, and 105 are significantly longer than 101 and 103.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. Step 2: Determine Root Causes: Case 102: Escalated to Level-2. ... Case 105: ... Common Patterns: Escalations: Cases 102 and 105 involve escalation. Both are long. Overnight Gaps: All slow cases have significant gaps spanning across days. Level-1 Bottleneck: ... Level-2 Bottleneck: ...

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 5. Step 3: Explain Factors & Propose Recommendations: Factor 1: Escalation Process. Escalated tickets take 2-4x longer. Factor 2: Business Hours/Shift Work. ... Factor 3: Triage/Assignment Latency. ... Recommendations: Improve Level-2 capacity or SLAs. Implement 24/7 support or on-call rotation for critical tickets. Standardize triage times. Analyze why tickets need escalation.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 6. Drafting the Response: Structure the answer clearly according to the prompts three requirements. ... Refinement on Calculations: 101: 08:00 to 10:15 = 2h 15m. ... Average of fast cases (101, 103): ~1h 50m. Average of slow cases (102, 104, 105): ~33 hours. Comparison: Slow cases are roughly 18x slower than fast cases. Specific Bottlenecks: Escalation Wait Time: ... Assignment Latency: ...

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: 7. Final Review: Does this answer all parts of the prompt? 1. Identify long cases? Yes (102, 104, 105). 2. Root causes? Yes ... 3. Recommendations? Yes. Wait, one detail check: Case 104 does not have an escalation but is still slow. ... Re-evaluating Case 104: Receive (08:20) -> Triage (09:00) [40m wait] ...
